Client Proposal: Personal insurance
Prepared by:

Your proposal

Consult would like to thank you for this opportunity to assist you with your financial planning portfolio. Consult by Momentum is a reputable financial planning and advisory company, and our passion is financial advice. Our goal is to assist you with a financial plan to meet your objectives. We will partner with you on your financial journey providing you with peace of mind as you embrace your future with confidence.

You can expect professional and unbiased financial advice from your Consult Adviser. We partner with advisers who share our vision and passion for financial advice by fostering long-term relationships with you and live by our values of honesty and integrity.

This proposal forms part of your overall financial planning map and should be a departure point for further dialogue.

When formulating our recommendations, we have considered your unique financial position, circumstances, and objectives that you have shared with us during the financial planning and advice process. Several variables affect our proposal, not least of which are our assumptions with respect to your circumstances, needs, risk information and objectives.

We encourage regular communication to keep us abreast of your circumstances so that our recommended solution is always relevant and appropriate.

Consult short term philosophy

Our short-term philosophy is centred around crafting tailored asset-protection solutions that support your personal needs and business strategy. We combine cutting-edge technology and professional advice, to offer appropriate solutions to suit your unique circumstances to ultimately protect your assets and give your peace of mind.

Our commitment
  • Providing advice with confidence
  • Offer a choice of reputable business partners
  • Active partnership throughout the lifecycle of your policy, ensuring relevance
  • Effective risk mitigating and management solutions
  • Place you in a position to make an informed decision
Our unique approach to crafting your solution

At Consult our advice philosophy considers your personal tangible assets as well as your intangible risks, we therefore review and solve for exposure to your assets and liability risks.

Professional Induction
Understanding your unique circumstances and needs
Analyse and prepare your ideal solution
Present and recommend
Agree and implement, based on your unique circumstances and needs
Annually review and refine the relevance of our products

Client service agreement

During the financial planning and advice process, you requested me to give advice on the following:

Personal insurance (Your personal assets): Personal assets refers to all the tangible assets that you own, this includes your personal vehicles, property, including your holiday home, all the items inside your home, as well as items that you remove from your property. In addition, personal assets also refer to recreational items such as caravans, trailers, and watercrafts.

I will not address any needs that fall outside of the above, but can also assist you with:

Commercial insurance (Your business assets): General commercial is a type of insurance coverage designed to protect businesses and commercial enterprises from a wide range of risks and liabilities. This includes, retail businesses, hospitality industry, professional services (doctors, law firms, architect), sports facilities.

Agri insurance (Your agricultural assets): Agricultural asset protection refers to all property, farming utensils, employees, livestock.

Specialist insurance (Unique business risk exposure): Specialist lines asset protection refers to coverage for unique business exposure; Engineering, Guarantees, Marine, Cyber risks.

This Proposal is based on your personal and financial information that was provided by you. Please take the time to ensure that all the information is correct, as the success of the plan and the appropriateness of the advice depend on it.

Your position: Personal insurance

Short-term insurance protects your personal belongings and assets against life’s unpredictable events, giving you piece of mind that you are adequately covered.

Summary of your circumstances, financial situation, needs and objectives

You have shared the following objectives with me during the financial planning and advice process. We assumed that this information is a 100% accurate and used it as basis for our calculations and assumptions, contained in this Proposal.

AI-generated recommendation — please review and edit before use.
Preset option 1 (selected): During our discussion, you mentioned that you are you seeking comprehensive cover for losses or damages to your residential and personal property. You would like your household content to be covered in the event of theft, malicious and accidental damage, including fire. You want cover for your personal belongings that you carry with you when you travel if the items are lost, stolen or damaged. You want comprehensive motor cover. This must include liability cover for third party damages that safeguards you from incidents that might arise. You mentioned that premium is not an issue and having an excess is an option for you.

Adviser note: The client is value-driven; premium is not the primary driver. Excess appetite is flexible, but an excess waiver is a stated hard requirement. Record the thatch-roof risk factor. Two vehicles are insured: Audi A6 (JG33GFGP) and VW Tiguan Allspace. The client is interested in unspecified all-risk cover.

Your excess structures

The excess structure is a pre-determined amount that is payable by yourself, before the insurer settles your claim. Refer to the specific excess structure of each asset type listed above as defined in your current policy schedule.

Standard excess

The first amount payable in the event of a claim refers to the default excess structure as defined by the insurer.

Voluntary excess

This is a selected excess amount that you are willing to pay in the event of a claim.

No excess

There is no first amount payable in the event of a claim.

Note: Your specific excess structure is defined in your current policy schedule, if applicable.

Product features

The product provider, cover, additional value-added products (VAPs) and optional extensions that I have recommended has certain benefits, features, terms, conditions and limitations or exclusions. These terms and conditions are disclosed in your product provider quotation(s) for your evaluation and consideration. It is important to understand these benefits, features, terms, conditions, and limitations, as they determine the circumstances under which claims are paid.

Motivation of advice provided

I am providing you with additional motivation for the advice provided, including reasoning and approach, which will most likely satisfy your financial needs and objectives.

AI-generated recommendation — please review and edit before use.
Preset option 1 (selected): Based on your unique circumstances risk information and insurance profile / insurance risk information, as well as objectives you shared throughout the advice process, I have considered specific insurance providers as well as ancillary products to formulate the ideal insurance solution that caters for comprehensive cover on your personal assets. Of the XX insurers that were considered, I believe Product Provider Name product is ideal. Product Provider Name product provides cover on an ALL-RISK basis, which aligns to your existing solution. The excess structure provided is based on default values and no percentage excesses are applicable on this solution.Product Provider Name does have similar conditions of cover to that of your existing insurer, with one addition that you install an alarm system with armed response. (please refer to your policy wording for a detailed view on your conditions of cover). Product Provider Name product covers all your personal assets as per your existing insurance solution, with the addition of assets you wished to include in this proposal. These assets have been insured for the values as per the risk information you provided. To enhance your insurance solution, I have included an excess waiver on all your assets, you therefore will not have an excess payable in the event of a claim. To ensure that the solution caters for non-tangible risks such as Cyber-attacks, I have included a cyber insurance solution that protects you and your immediate family.

Adviser note: Replace 'Product Provider Name' with Renasa (Executive). Correct three points before use:
1. The Renasa quote does NOT include a cyber solution.
2. Percentage (10%) excesses DO apply on several sections.
3. The excess waiver is a basic-excess waiver limited to insured/spouse aged 55 and older.
Also confirm the Renasa policy basis (peril vs all-risk). This is not stated on the quote and cannot be verified, as no Renasa wording is available.

Affordability

If you are unable to implement the full recommendation due to affordability, discuss and specify what you are willing to spend, or provide your financial adviser with an accurate statement of your budget, to substantiate your affordability.

I will provide you with appropriate advice and recommend a suitable product solution to address your circumstances, needs, and objectives per event. The purpose of the recommendation is to put you in a position to make an informed decision.

It is important to engage with your financial adviser when your financial situation changes to ensure that the impact and risk are appropriately addressed.

You indicated the following during our discussion regarding affordability:

AI-generated recommendation — please review and edit before use.
Preset option 1 (selected): You indicated you are willing to spend within the following range monthly:

Adviser note: The Renasa premium is R7,339.48 pm versus the Hollard premium of R3,715.36 pm. That is an increase of about R3,624 pm (+97.6%). It is not strictly like-for-like: the Renasa quote adds Accidental Damage, All Risks and Extended Personal Liability sections. Confirm the client's stated monthly budget range accommodates the higher premium.

Our fee structures

Refer to this proposal and all related annexures, including the product quotation from the relevant product provider(s) and the policy fee agreement for a full overview and the exact nature of all direct and indirect fees applicable for your recommended solution.

Fees agreed with your financial adviser are for the financial advice and services provided by your financial adviser. This includes annual reviews, ongoing services, introducing you to the different product offerings, as you may require from time to time.

Regulated commission, referral fees and outsource fees

Adviser commission
Referral fees (if applicable)
Outsource fees (if applicable)

Policy Fees

In terms of legislation, your financial adviser may charge fees for additional services rendered over and above intermediary services, for which regulated commission is payable, provided such fees are expressly and separately disclosed to the policyholder and provided that your financial adviser has not already been remunerated for these services by an insurer or another third party. These fees are usually referred to as policy fees in the short term insurance industry and must be agreed upon by the policyholder. An intermediary service as defined in the FAIS Act can be summarised as any act other than the furnishing of advice performed by a person on behalf of a client or product supplier which results in a client purchasing, amending, or renewing an insurance policy. Also included in the definition of services of an intermediary is collecting or accounting for premiums; receiving, submitting, or processing the claims of a client; and client servicing.

Service description

Your financial adviser will render the following additional services to you, which are not considered "intermediary services" and for which your financial adviser is not remunerated by the insurer or any other third party.

Selected relevant non-intermediary services

Access to Client engagement platform (mobile application linked to franchise operations)
Assist with arranging valuations / assessment of loss with suitable professionals
Advice outside the ambit of financial products e.g., Risk advice and risk management services
Inspection of commercial sites for risk assessment
On-site visits when requested and with policy renewal
Arranging online building / premises surveys
Additional costs incurred to determine value of insured assets
Assistance in the event of an accident or claims event such as arrangement of towing or breakdown services
Facilitation of non-insurance value added products
Fee amount:
Frequency:

Policy fees are commensurate with the services rendered and may be cancelled on request of the client at any time should they not require the additional value-added services.

General terms and conditions disclaimer

Consult short-term insurance: general terms and conditions disclaimer – personal insurance
General conditions that may be applicable to your insurance solution
  • Note that you have a responsibility to inform your financial adviser of any material changes to your insurance portfolio.
  • A material change is any fact that affects the nature of the risk which is covered by the insurance policy. e.g., a change of risk address/alterations to security systems and construction types / change in regular driver.
  • An excess is the first amount payable in the event of a claim, excesses may be different for each section of your policy, therefore familiarize yourself with your unique excess structure. You may qualify for an excess reducer, discuss these options with your financial adviser.
  • An excess is the first amount payable in the event of a claim, excesses may be different for each section of your policy, therefore familiarize yourself with your unique excess structure. You may qualify for an excess reducer, discuss these options with your financial adviser.
  • All claims must be reported within 30- days or as per the insurance company wording, you must immediately report to the police any event where theft or any other criminal act is involved.
  • You must take all reasonable precautions and all reasonable care to prevent or minimise loss, damage, death, injury, or liability. Failure to apply reasonable care may affect the outcome of your claim.
  • Note that your insurer reserves the right to replace, repair, cash settlement or a combination of these to indemnify you for the losses.
  • Most insurance contracts run on a month-to-month basis and therefore it’s your responsibility to ensure that your insurance premiums are paid on time as insurers have the right to repudiate your claim in the event of a failed debit. Some insurers may allow a grace period, whereafter a second attempt to collect funds will be processed.
  • Please note that laptops and other computer equipment must be securely locked by means of a cable lock when left unattended. Similarly, these items must be kept in a securely locked compartment of a vehicle when the vehicle is left unattended. Failure to comply with these requirements could result in your claim being repudiated.
  • It is good practice to complete an inventory form of all your belongings at the risk address, this may aid you in determining the correct insured value of your belongings and in doing so avoid law of average being applied by your insurer.
  • Law of average may be applied by the insurer if the assessor finds your property or contents to be underinsured; the value of the property or belongings being insured has been understated and therefore the contractual premium offered by the insurer is not aligned to the correct exposure value. The insurer in this scenario may only pay a proportion of the loss and the insured will therefore have to bear the remaining portion himself for e.g.:
  • Actual Value of Property /Contents: R50 000 Insured Amount: R30 000 Claim amount: R20 000
  • Amount payable: (Sum Insured /Actual Value of Property /Contents) x Claim Amount (R30000/ R50 000) x R20000 = 12 000 Payable
  • It is your responsibility to make sure that you are adequately insured to avoid law of average being applied.
  • Lack of maintenance to your building, roof structure, retaining walls as well as any other material risks at the insured premises could result in the repudiation of your claim. It is advisable that regular maintenance is conducted, and that proof of such maintenance is shared with your financial adviser to safeguard.
  • If an alarm is a condition of cover on your policy; the alarm system must be maintained and kept in always working order and activated when the property is unoccupied. In the event of a burglary, your insurer is entitled to request proof from your security company that these requirements have been complied with.
  • Dual insurance may lead to proportionate settlement of loss, its therefore important to avoid covering the same risk on two policies.
  • The regular driver status of a vehicle is a key risk factor to determining your insurance premium and acceptance, incorrect information may lead to the repudiation of your claim.
  • Ensure that the correct use of vehicle is disclosed to your financial adviser, as incorrect usage may result in your claim being repudiated. Your financial adviser would be able to guide you in terms of the limits and types of usage.
  • All vehicle accessories and extras that are not factory-fitted should be disclosed to your financial adviser for e.g., sunroof, alloy wheels, tow bar, etc. It’s your responsibility to ensure that the sum insured values are provided to your financial adviser.
  • You are advised to review your insurance policy schedule in conjunction with your policy wording to familiarise yourself with the terms and conditions, also consider all optional covers or extensions available to you on your policy, should you require any additional cover, please notify your financial adviser.

I confirm that this proposal and annexures has been explained to me and that I understand the contents thereof.

I confirm that a full overview and the exact nature of all direct and indirect fees applicable for my recommended solution(s) and related annexures has been explained to me and that I understand the contents thereof.

I accept the advice provided to me as outlined in this proposal.

Considering the recommendations outlined in this proposal, based on my circumstances, individual needs and insurance risk information, affordability, and priorities, one of the following options has been agreed to for final implementation:

Full implementation

Partial implementation

No implementation

If the full recommendation is NOT implemented, provide the reason for NO/PARTIAL implementation. Clarify the purpose of implemented solution(s), shortfalls, risk, and impact facing the client as indicated in above applicable section.

1. Hollard prices the current cover at R3,715.36 pm. The client is seeking a materially wider itemised benefit set (All Risks, Extended Personal Liability, explicit Accidental Damage) that the existing structure does not provide.
2. Whether Hollard can add unspecified all-risk cover and the additional liability extensions without re-rating to a comparable premium.
3. Whether the client's value-for-money objective is better met by moving product than by amending the existing Hollard policy.
2. Reason(s) why the replacement product was more suitable to your insurance needs and objectives
AI-generated recommendation — please review and edit before use.
This section is to be completed by the adviser. Consider:
1. Renasa adds an unspecified All Risks section (R250,000), Extended Personal Legal Liability (R12,000,000) and explicit Accidental Damage cover not itemised on the Hollard policy.
2. Renasa itemises a wide range of buildings, contents and personal-liability extensions (e.g. Wrongful Arrest, Domestic Employees, Tenants Liability, leak detection, credit shortfall).
3. The Renasa Personal Liability limit rises to R15,000,000 with additional liability extensions.
4. A basic-excess waiver applies for insured/spouse aged 55 and older across the main sections.

AI-generated recommendation — please review and edit before use.
  1. Warranties (Thatch/Wood Construction; Jewelry/Watch left unattended; Apartments; Free Standing House / Holiday Flats security requirements; High Security Area definition) - theft cover subject to compliance with stated security warranties. See pages 5-6.
  2. Section Endorsement - Tracking Requirement / Security Requirements for Theft and Hijack Cover (valid from 23 March 2026): all insured vehicles must have a VESA/VSS approved security system. Listed high-theft vehicles without a tracking device attract an additional 20% theft/hijack excess. Non-compliance penalty: additional 20% excess on theft/hijack claims. Waived where a Pulsit SVR Plus unit is fitted and working at time of loss.
